What it contains: Historical observation parquet data, partitioned by UTC day and connector. Contains day manifests, connector manifests, and part-xxxxx.parquet files.
Where it is used: Read by the Observs History R2 API Worker for old timeseries data. Also used by backfill modes that rebuild AQI from observation history.

What it contains: One committed UTC day of observations. Has a day-level manifest.json, connector subfolders, and parquet files.
Where it is used: This is the main backup unit copied to Dropbox. Readers treat the day manifest as the source of truth for a complete committed day.

What it contains: Observation parquet parts and connector-level manifest.json for one connector on one UTC day.
Where it is used: Used by R2 readers and by the timeseries index builder/reader to know which files contain which timeseries ranges.

What it contains: Historical AQI-level parquet data, also partitioned by UTC day and connector.
Where it is used: Used for AQI history, retention checks, and backup. Backfill can rewrite AQI from observations history.

What it contains: One committed UTC day of AQI data. Has day manifest, connector manifests, and parquet parts.
Where it is used: AQI retention checks look for committed day manifests here before deleting old local AQI data. Dropbox backup mirrors this layout.

What it contains: Daily core snapshots: metadata/reference tables as compressed NDJSON plus checksums and manifest.
Where it is used: Used for restoring/reconciling historical context, e.g. mapping Sensor.Community archive rows using local core snapshots.

What it contains: One daily core snapshot. Contains manifest.json, checksums.sha256, and table=/rows.ndjson.gz.
Where it is used: Copied by the same backup script as a third domain, although your recent run copied 0 core days.

What it contains: Derived index objects. Includes latest summary manifests and timeseries file-range index trees.
Where it is used: Used for fast listing/history-days paths and to reduce R2 reader work by narrowing parquet scans. The Dropbox backup mirrors these too.

What it contains: Latest summary/index manifest for observation history.
Where it is used: Copied by hash comparison in the backup script. Used by dashboard/history-days style readers.

What it contains: Latest summary/index manifest for AQI history.
Where it is used: Same as above, but for AQI.

What it contains: Descriptor for the observations timeseries file-range index.
Where it is used: Used to know coverage and locate per-day/per-connector timeseries index manifests.

What it contains: Lightweight file-range index for one observation day+connector. Lists parquet files and their timeseries_id/time ranges.
Where it is used: The Observs History R2 API Worker can use this to avoid scanning irrelevant parquet files.

What it contains: Descriptor for the AQI timeseries file-range index.
Where it is used: Copied by backup and used for AQI history acceleration.

What it contains: Lightweight file-range index for one AQI day+connector. Includes file ranges and pollutant availability.
Where it is used: Used to narrow AQI parquet reads.

What it contains: Operational run manifests from Phase B export runs.
Where it is used: Audit/ops trail; not the main public committed history.
Cleanup/drainage only; current observation export writes committed parts directly.

What it contains: Backup checkpoint state stored in Dropbox, not R2 History itself. Tracks copied day manifest hashes.
Where it is used: Used by sync_history_to_dropbox.mjs to decide whether a day is new, unchanged, or changed.
